tldr: Headlines across all 12 buckets foreground Russia's escalating missile and drone assault on Ukrainian cities — Kyiv, Sumy, Odesa, Kharkiv — with a parallel thread of Ukrainian drone counterstrikes hitting Russian territory, oil tankers, and Black Sea vessels; several headlines contextualise the attacks against the backdrop of a Ukraine-EU drone deal or an imminent NATO summit.

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline to foreground Russia's air-defense success ('down 375 drones') as the lead fact, minimising civilian harm and inverting the Ukrainian-aggressor framing dominant in Western and neutral outlets.

Only headline to label Russia's ballistic missile campaign 'terror' by quoting Zelensky directly — the sole outlet to deploy explicit moral/rhetorical characterisation rather than neutral strike-count reporting.

Only headline to invoke legal language ('No Piracy, Lavrov') — rebutting Russia's characterisation of Ukraine's Black Sea drone campaign and framing the maritime fight as a matter of lawful warfare rather than criminal conduct.

bottom_line: The headline layer presents the Russia-Ukraine conflict as an active, intensifying aerial exchange — Russia battering Ukrainian cities with missiles and drones, Ukraine striking back at Russian territory, oil infrastructure, and Black Sea tankers — while diplomatic context (EU-Ukraine drone deal, NATO summit, EU chief's visit) surfaces only as background colour in three outlier headlines.
